headline: Traders Ready to Put War Behind Them Dial Up Risk

headline: How The Callaway Golf (CALY) Investment Narrative Is Shifting With Mixed Analyst Targets
summary: Callaway Golf is back in focus as analysts fine tune their price targets, including moves such as JPMorgan’s shift from US$16 to US$15, while a fair value anchor of US$16.75 remains in place in one framework. These updates reflect a mix of views on recent market share trends, the strength of golf spending, and how much of a recovery story is already reflected in current pricing. As you read on, you will see how these shifting targets fit into a broader narrative and how to track the key...

headline: Iran war, $4 gas, took all the fun out of the U.S. consumer economy. There's only so much time to bring it back
summary: Consumers are still spending amid U.S.-Iran war and $4 gas prices, but there's a pullback at entertainment and dining venues hitting local economies hard.

headline: How The Investment Narrative For Pitney Bowes (PBI) Is Shifting With Buybacks And 2026 Guidance
summary: The updated work on Pitney Bowes includes a modest lift in the fair value price target to US$12.70 from US$12.50. That compares with a backdrop where bullish analysts are discussing targets around US$9.50 and paying closer attention to how buybacks and operating trends could influence future outcomes. As you read on, you will see how these differing views fit together and what to watch as the narrative continues to evolve. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over...

headline: The Bull Case For Bank of America (BAC) Could Change Following Record Q1 Earnings And Higher NII Guidance
summary: Bank of America reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, with net interest income rising to US$15,745 million and net income reaching US$8,584 million, while also continuing to actively manage its balance sheet through new senior note issuances and the early redemption of existing fixed/floating rate debt. The bank highlighted record equities trading, double-digit gains in investment banking and asset management fees, and growing use of AI tools across its workforce as key contributors to...

headline: Bank of America goes all in on controversial tech
summary: Banks have been embracing AI recently, despite warnings from the federal government. Regulators have told banks that a new AI model could pose a significant security risk. But not all banks seem to be heeding these warnings, as nearly 70% of banks use AI in their operations, according to The ...
